title: Gaffrey Lab Infant Microstates Reliability
description: This dataset comprises continuous EEG recordings from 48 infants (5-10 months old) during passive video viewing, designed to establish reliability metrics for microstate analysis in infant populations. Participants watched relaxing videos while EEG was recorded using a 128-channel geodesic sensor net at 1000 Hz. The dataset serves as a tutorial resource and validation study for microstate analysis methodology in developmental neuroscience.
methods_description: EEG was recorded at 1000 Hz using a 128-channel HydroCel Geodesic Sensor Net referenced to vertex (Cz), with impedances maintained below 50 kΩ. Infants (N=48, 5-10 months, 27 male) sat on caregiver's lap and viewed up to 15 minutes of relaxing videos (ten 90-second videos with breaks) preceded by attention-grabbing stimuli. Videos were presented using E-Prime software.
